response.justification: The candidate is an experienced senior software developer with strong full-stack and DLang expertise, but the role specifically requires a C++ developer with 8-12 years of focused C++ experience, industrial automation background, and IEC 62443/SDL security practices. The resume shows only a brief 6-month stint writing C/C++ at National Instruments over a decade ago (2014-2015), with no evidence of recent C++ work, industrial automation, MQTT/Modbus/OPC UA protocols, or IEC 62443 secure development practices. Core competencies required for this role are essentially absent.
strengths: Strong overall software engineering experience (~13 years) with testing, TDD, and CI/CD craftsmanship
Background in event-driven architectures, microservices, and high-throughput systems (Sociomantic)
Mentorship and teaching experience via workshops, courses, and Refactoring Dojos
concerns: Only ~6 months of C/C++ experience, from 2014-2015 — no recent C++ or STL work
No exposure to industrial automation, IEC 62443, SDL, or industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850)
C++ is not listed in the skills section; primary stack is JS/TS and DLang

key_factors.factor: No industrial automation or IEC 62443 security background
Strong general software craftsmanship and architecture experience
